description: Codex-themed release: provider-neutral setup core, provider-aware first-run prompting, Transcript-first drawer surface, provider-aware Context, terminal resize polish, and release-copy cleanup are in place.

items.before: The Context tab is still explicitly Claude-specific. Its backend collector is hard-wired to `CLAUDE.md`, one-hop `@` imports, `.claude/hooks`, `.claude/settings*.json`, and `~/.claude/projects/.../memory`, and the UI/docs describe it that way. For a Codex-themed release this leaves an obvious gap: when Codex is the active provider, the app still shows Claude's context model instead of the durable Codex-side sources that actually shape a Codex session on this machine.
items.after: Make Context provider-aware. When Claude is active, keep the current Claude-oriented view. When Codex is active, show a Codex-oriented context view built from durable local sources such as `~/.codex/config.toml`, project trust/config entries, `~/.codex/memories/`, and `~/.codex/rules/` when present. Update the UI and docs so Context plainly reflects the active provider's real context model instead of implying Claude's `CLAUDE.md` import chain applies to Codex too. Backend: `collect_codex_context_files` reads `~/.codex/config.toml`, project-local `.codex/` files, `~/.codex/memories/`, and `~/.codex/rules/`; `collect_claude_context_files` keeps the existing Claude-specific tree; `context_provider` picks the collector based on the active session hint, falling back to Claude. `context_list` returns a generic `{ provider, summary, sections: [{ key, label, items }] }` envelope (Project/Memory/Hooks/Settings for Claude; Project/Memories/Rules for Codex). Considered (a) one collector that always reads both Claude and Codex sources and labels them per-section — rejected because it implied both providers' context applies simultaneously; (b) leaving the UI Claude-only and adding a separate Codex tab — rejected because Sessions already demonstrates the provider-aware pattern and a doubled tab would split attention. UI: `Context.xmlui` now passes `activeProvider` to both `/__context/list` and `/__context/search`, clears the right-pane selection when the provider flips, renders a single `Items` over `sections`, and shows the per-provider `summary` under the Context heading. `Main.xmlui` wires `activeProvider` into the Context page. README: the agent-tools entry now describes Context as provider-aware and names the Claude vs. Codex sources explicitly.

items.before: When the terminal is resized while running Codex, the viewport jumps to the top. The resize path in xmlui-desktop is shared across providers (`fitAddon.fit()` plus `pty_resize`), so this looks less like a provider-specific code branch and more like an interaction between Codex's TUI redraw behavior and how aggressively the host resizes the terminal during window and splitter changes. Claude does not exhibit the same visible jump, which makes the current behavior feel especially rough in a Codex-themed release.
items.after: Polish terminal resizing so Codex does not jump the viewport to the top during window or splitter resizes. Preserve or restore the visible xterm viewport around `fitAddon.fit()`, and debounce resize traffic enough to avoid flooding Codex with redraw-triggering size changes while dragging. Keep the existing provider-agnostic PTY plumbing, but make the resize experience stable enough that Codex feels first-class in the terminal pane. Frontend: `app/main.js` now wraps terminal fitting in `captureViewport` / `restoreViewport`, uses `scheduleTerminalFit` to coalesce repeated `fit()` requests to one per animation frame, and uses a throttled `flushPtyResize` path so `pty_resize` calls are not fired on every intermediate drag tick. Window resize, terminal show/hide, splitter drag, and font-size changes all route through the same viewport-preserving fit path. README: the terminal toolbar bullet now says window/splitter resizes preserve the terminal viewport instead of snapping scrollback to the top.

items.before: After the Codex-focused feature work landed, some user-facing copy still reflected the older Claude-leaning UI. The built-in welcome page still described Context as what Claude loads, the README still implied the drawer voice/screenshot controls were visible in the current UI, and session notifications still told users to restart Claude specifically. That left the release copy lagging behind the actual Codex-themed product surface.
items.after: Clean up the remaining release-facing copy so the docs and in-app messaging match the current Codex-themed UI. README now describes the Claude-side convention path more precisely, no longer implies hidden drawer voice/screenshot controls are still exposed, and explains the screenshot helper as currently hidden in the default UI. The built-in welcome page now describes Context as provider-aware rather than Claude-only. Session notifications now tell users to restart the agent rather than Claude specifically.

items.before: Transcript already renders Claude tool_use/tool_result activity as inline tool cards with expandable details, which is one of the most useful parts of the live transcript. Codex sessions, however, are currently flattened to `event_msg` user/agent text only. The actual Codex session JSONL contains richer records such as `response_item` entries of type `function_call`, `custom_tool_call`, `function_call_output`, and `custom_tool_call_output`, with call ids, tool names, arguments, and outputs. Because `sessionTurns()` and `getToolDetail()` in `app/tools/Globals.xs` only understand Claude's `message.content` tool blocks, Codex tool activity is invisible in Transcript even though the underlying session data is present.
items.after: Teach Transcript to render Codex tool activity with the same general affordances Claude already gets: inline tool cards in the turn stream, error tinting when the tool failed, and expandable detail showing arguments and output. Implementation: extend the Codex parsing path in `app/tools/Globals.xs` so `response_item` `function_call` / `custom_tool_call` records become `kind: 'tool'` entries keyed by `call_id`, and their matching `function_call_output` / `custom_tool_call_output` records enrich the same entry with result/error state. `getToolDetail()` should become provider-aware enough to recover Codex call arguments and output payloads on expand, not just Claude `tool_use` / `tool_result` blocks. `Transcript.xmlui` should keep the existing renderer shape if possible, only widening the modal/detail formatting where Codex tool payloads differ. README should update the Transcript description to say that active-session tool activity is shown for both Claude and Codex rather than implying Claude-only richness.
